req.title: Fraud alert response (negative variant)
req.statement: A customer shall confirm or deny a suspected-fraud alert within 24 hours; unacknowledged alerts shall auto-freeze the account. Negative paths (insufficient funds, unauthorised, exceeded limits) must be tested.

doc.reasoning: The requirement mandates: (1) a customer can confirm or deny a fraud alert within 24 h and the account is unaffected; (2) an unacknowledged alert after 24 h triggers auto-freeze; (3) a response to an already-expired alert returns 410. Negative paths include unauthorised access, insufficient funds context, and exceeded limits. Test design covers: EP for the happy-path confirm/deny cycle, STC for the alert state machine (active→closed, active→frozen), NEG for the expired-alert 410, BVA for the 24-hour boundary, and DT for the unauthorised-caller variant. Each test is self-contained via fixtures that create and, where needed, fast-forward or expire an alert.

tests.name: test_confirm_fraud_alert_closes_alert_and_reissues_card
preconditions: A fraud alert exists in ACTIVE state for the authenticated customer
Customer has a valid session token with alerts:write scope
Alert was raised within the last 24 hours
actions: Assert HTTP 200 and alert status transitions to CLOSED
Assert card_reissued flag is true in response body
trace: R-FIN-063: card reissued if fraud confirmed
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_confirm_fraud_alert_closes_alert_and_reissues_card(
    active_alert_fixture,
    auth_headers_fixture,
    banking_api_base_url,
):
    alert_id = active_alert_fixture["id"]
    url = f"{banking_api_base_url}/alerts/{alert_id}"

    resp = requests.patch(
        url,
        json={"response": "confirm"},
        headers=auth_headers_fixture,
    )

    assert resp.status_code == 200, f"Expected 200, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "closed", f"Alert not closed: {body}"
    assert body["card_reissued"] is True, f"Card not reissued: {body}"

    get_resp = requests.get(url, headers=auth_headers_fixture)
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    assert get_resp.json()["status"] == "closed"

tests.name: test_deny_fraud_alert_closes_alert_without_card_reissue
preconditions: A fraud alert exists in ACTIVE state for the authenticated customer
Customer has a valid session token with alerts:write scope
Alert was raised within the last 24 hours
actions: Assert HTTP 200 and alert status transitions to CLOSED
Assert card_reissued flag is false in response body
trace: R-FIN-063: card NOT reissued when fraud denied
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_deny_fraud_alert_closes_alert_without_card_reissue(
    active_alert_fixture,
    auth_headers_fixture,
    banking_api_base_url,
):
    alert_id = active_alert_fixture["id"]
    url = f"{banking_api_base_url}/alerts/{alert_id}"

    resp = requests.patch(
        url,
        json={"response": "deny"},
        headers=auth_headers_fixture,
    )

    assert resp.status_code == 200, f"Expected 200, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "closed", f"Alert not closed: {body}"
    assert body["card_reissued"] is False, f"Card unexpectedly reissued: {body}"

    get_resp = requests.get(url, headers=auth_headers_fixture)
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    assert get_resp.json()["status"] == "closed"

tests.name: test_expired_alert_returns_410
preconditions: A fraud alert exists that was raised MORE than 24 hours ago
Alert is in AUTO_FROZEN state (system auto-froze account)
Customer has a valid session token with alerts:write scope
actions: PATCH /alerts/{id} with body {"response": "confirm"} for the expired alert
Assert error code in body is ALERT_EXPIRED
expected: Account remains in FROZEN state (no state regression)
trace: R-FIN-063: error pathway 410 when alert expired
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_expired_alert_returns_410(
    expired_alert_fixture,
    auth_headers_fixture,
    banking_api_base_url,
):
    alert_id = expired_alert_fixture["id"]
    url = f"{banking_api_base_url}/alerts/{alert_id}"

    resp = requests.patch(
        url,
        json={"response": "confirm"},
        headers=auth_headers_fixture,
    )

    assert resp.status_code == 410, f"Expected 410, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("error_code") == "ALERT_EXPIRED", f"Unexpected error body: {body}"

    acct_resp = requests.get(
        f"{banking_api_base_url}/accounts/{expired_alert_fixture['account_id']}",
        headers=auth_headers_fixture,
    )
    assert acct_resp.json()["status"] == "frozen"

tests.name: test_alert_auto_freezes_at_24_hour_boundary
preconditions: A fraud alert exists that was raised exactly 24 hours ago (boundary instant)
Alert has NOT yet been acknowledged
System clock or test-clock can be advanced to T+24h
actions: Advance test clock to T+24h via <advance_clock_fixture>
Trigger the auto-freeze job via POST /internal/alerts/process-expired (test helper)
expected: Account status transitions to FROZEN at T+24h
trace: R-FIN-063: unacknowledged alerts auto-freeze the account
R-FIN-063: 24-hour deadline is the boundary value
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_alert_auto_freezes_at_24_hour_boundary(
    active_alert_fixture,
    auth_headers_fixture,
    banking_api_base_url,
    advance_clock_fixture,
    internal_auth_headers_fixture,
):
    alert_id = active_alert_fixture["id"]
    account_id = active_alert_fixture["account_id"]

    advance_clock_fixture(hours=24)

    proc_resp = requests.post(
        f"{banking_api_base_url}/internal/alerts/process-expired",
        headers=internal_auth_headers_fixture,
    )
    assert proc_resp.status_code in (200, 204)

    acct_resp = requests.get(
        f"{banking_api_base_url}/accounts/{account_id}",
        headers=auth_headers_fixture,
    )
    assert acct_resp.json()["status"] == "frozen", f"Account not frozen: {acct_resp.json()}"

    patch_resp = requests.patch(
        f"{banking_api_base_url}/alerts/{alert_id}",
        json={"response": "confirm"},
        headers=auth_headers_fixture,
    )
    assert patch_resp.status_code == 410
    assert patch_resp.json().get("error_code") == "ALERT_EXPIRED"

tests.name: test_unauthorised_caller_cannot_respond_to_alert
preconditions: A fraud alert exists in ACTIVE state
Caller presents credentials belonging to a DIFFERENT customer (not the alert owner)
actions: PATCH /alerts/{id} with body {"response": "confirm"} using wrong customer token
Assert alert remains in ACTIVE state via authorised GET
expected: Alert status remains ACTIVE (no state mutation)
trace: R-FIN-063: only the owning customer may respond
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_unauthorised_caller_cannot_respond_to_alert(
    active_alert_fixture,
    auth_headers_fixture,
    other_customer_auth_headers_fixture,
    banking_api_base_url,
):
    alert_id = active_alert_fixture["id"]
    url = f"{banking_api_base_url}/alerts/{alert_id}"

    resp = requests.patch(
        url,
        json={"response": "confirm"},
        headers=other_customer_auth_headers_fixture,
    )

    assert resp.status_code == 403, f"Expected 403, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("error_code") == "FORBIDDEN", f"Unexpected error body: {body}"

    get_resp = requests.get(url, headers=auth_headers_fixture)
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    assert get_resp.json()["status"] == "active", f"Alert mutated unexpectedly: {get_resp.json()}"

tests.name: test_alert_response_with_invalid_payload_returns_422
preconditions: A fraud alert exists in ACTIVE state for the authenticated customer
Customer has a valid session token with alerts:write scope
actions: Assert HTTP 422 Unprocessable Entity is returned
Assert alert status is still ACTIVE
expected: Response body contains validation error referencing "response" field
trace: R-FIN-063: negative path — exceeded/invalid request data
R-FIN-063: only confirm or deny are valid responses
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_alert_response_with_invalid_payload_returns_422(
    active_alert_fixture,
    auth_headers_fixture,
    banking_api_base_url,
):
    alert_id = active_alert_fixture["id"]
    url = f"{banking_api_base_url}/alerts/{alert_id}"

    resp = requests.patch(
        url,
        json={"response": "maybe"},
        headers=auth_headers_fixture,
    )

    assert resp.status_code == 422, f"Expected 422, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    errors = body.get("errors") or body.get("detail") or []
    error_str = str(errors)
    assert "response" in error_str.lower(), f"Validation error not about 'response': {body}"

    get_resp = requests.get(url, headers=auth_headers_fixture)
    assert get_resp.json()["status"] == "active"
